digest.en: US President Donald Trump extended a truce with Iran. Reported seizures of ships near the Strait of Hormuz added uncertainty to peace talks. The US is also withholding Iraq's oil money due to its ties with Iran.
digest.hi: संयुक्त राष्ट्र की एक वरिष्ठ अधिकारी ने कहा कि भारत ने इजराइल को हथियार भेजकर अंतरराष्ट्रीय नियमों का उल्लंघन किया है। इससे वैश्विक प्रणाली पर प्रभाव पड़ सकता है। अमेरिका ने युद्धविराम बढ़ाने की बात कही है, जिस पर अरब मीडिया में चर्चा हो रही है।

articles.title: To Iran, Trump Blinked First by Extending the Cease-Fire
articles.summary: Iran's leaders believe they can outlast President Trump in a standoff, which could devastate average Iranians economically. Iran’s leaders believe that they can withstand an enduring standoff longer than President Trump.

articles.title: Reported Seizure of Ships Injects New Uncertainty Over Peace Talks
articles.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards seized two cargo ships near the Strait of Hormuz, state media reported. The incident occurred as President Trump renewed a cease-fire, with both sides seeking control in the waterway.

articles.title: Iranian Forces Claim to Seize 2 Ships Near Strait of Hormuz After Trump Extends Truce
articles.summary: Iranian forces seized two ships near the Strait of Hormuz. The incident occurred after a cease-fire extension. Iran War Live Updates: Iranian Forces Claim Seizure of 2 Ships Near Strait of Hormuz After Trump Extends Cease-Fire The New York Times.

articles.title: Horror Russia-NATO war warning issued by European spy agency
articles.summary: A European spy agency has issued a warning about a potential war between Russia and NATO. The agency has raised concerns about the rising tensions between the two. Horror Russia-NATO war warning issued by European spy agency Daily Express.

articles.title: US Withholding Iraq’s Own Oil Money Over Ties to Iran
articles.summary: The US halted dollar shipments to Iraq's economy to force the government to distance itself from Iran. The United States has halted dollar shipments to Iraq’s cash-based economy in an effort to force the Baghdad government to distance itself from Iran.

articles.title: Three Ships Attacked in Strait of Hormuz, Escorting Two to Its Coast
articles.summary: Three ships were attacked in the Strait of Hormuz after the US extended a cease-fire and blockade. The incident occurred despite a 10-day truce brokered by the US, which is set to expire.

articles.title: 3 US Presidents Rejected Netanyahu War Plan, Trump Agreed: Ex-Top Official
articles.summary: Netanyahu convinced President Donald Trump to attack Iran alongside Israel, a plan rejected by three prior US presidents. Netanyahu convinced US President Donald Trump to attack Iran alongside Israel. 3 US Presidents Rejected Netanyahu War Plan, Trump Agreed: Ex-Top Official.

articles.title: Iran attacks ships in Strait of Hormuz as thousands more U.S. forces head for Middle East
articles.summary: Iran attacked ships in the Strait of Hormuz as thousands of US forces headed to the Middle East. Live Updates: Iran attacks ships in Strait of Hormuz as thousands more U. S.

articles.title: At least four killed in Israeli strikes on Lebanon, journalists wounded
articles.summary: Israeli strikes on Lebanon killed at least four people and wounded journalists in at-Tiri. Journalists wounded in Israeli attacks in at-Tiri, Lebanon's National News Agency (NNA) reports. At least four killed in Israeli strikes on Lebanon, journalists wounded.

articles.title: South Korean arrested outside Yasukuni Shrine ceremony for war dead
articles.summary: A South Korean national was arrested at Tokyo's Yasukuni Shrine for holding a banner with political messages. Japan's prime minister did not attend the ceremony. Police said a South Korean national holding a banner with political messages was arrested at Tokyo's Yasukuni Shrine to Japan's war dead.

articles.title: EU unblocks €90 billion Ukraine loan after Hungary row
articles.summary: The EU will unblock a €90 billion loan for Ukraine by April 23, 2026. Hungary awaits Russian oil through the Druzhba pipeline. Diplomats said the definitive sign-off should come by Thursday (April 23, 2026) as Budapest waits for Russian oil to arrive through the Druzhba pipeline after Kyiv said operations had restarted.

articles.title: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine as pipeline is turned on ending deadlock
articles.summary: Ukraine reopened the Druzhba pipeline, ending a months-long stalemate over oil supplies to Hungary. The EU approved a €90bn loan. Ukraine says it has reopened the Druzhba pipeline, after months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to neighbouring Hungary.

articles.title: Explainer-How Deutsche Telecom and T-Mobile US could pull off the world's biggest M&A deal
articles.summary: Deutsche Telecom and T-Mobile US are considering a merger. Details of the potential deal are not yet available. Explainer-How Deutsche Telecom and T-Mobile US could pull off the world's biggest M&A deal.

articles.title: Israeli strikes kill 3 in Lebanon, Beirut to seek truce extension
articles.summary: Israeli strikes in Lebanon killed 3 people. Lebanon will seek a truce extension in talks with Israel on April 23, 2026. Ahead of the talks on Thursday (April 23, 2026), Israel called on the Lebanese government to "work together" with it against Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah.

articles.title: What is stopping EU from holding Israel to account?
articles.summary: The European Union has suspended consideration of a lucrative association agreement with Israel due to trade, historical, and internal rifts. The move comes amid ongoing tensions between the EU and Israel over various issues.

articles.title: U.S.-Iran talks could be held in next three days, says Trump
articles.summary: US and Iran may hold talks within three days, President Trump said. Iran's Guards claim to have seized two ships attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz. Iran Guards say 'seized' two ships attempting to cross Strait of Hormuz; Iran says it has yet to decide whether to join a new round of talks with the U. S.

articles.title: British woman died in Ghana trying to recoup money from scammers, inquest told
articles.summary: Janet Fordham, a British woman, died in a road crash in Ghana after traveling to recoup money lost to romance scammers. She had been cheated out of up to £1m in a string of scams.

articles.title: Ukraine restarts Russian oil to Europe, unblocking 90-billion-euro EU loan
articles.summary: Ukraine has restarted oil deliveries to Europe, enabling Hungary to lift its veto on a 90-billion-euro EU loan. The move clears the way for the EU's 27 members to formally approve the loan.

articles.title: How Israel is destroying Lebanon’s water infrastructure
articles.summary: Israel is deliberately attacking Lebanon's water infrastructure, experts say, aiming to displace or kill the population in south Lebanon. The attacks have been ongoing for some time. Israel is 'deliberately' attacking Lebanon's water, experts say, aiming to displace or kill south Lebanon's population.

articles.title: EU Considers $106 Billion Loan to Ukraine, Delayed for Months by Orban
articles.summary: EU ambassadors meet to discuss a $106 billion loan to Ukraine, delayed by Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban's objections. After months of impasse because of objections from Prime Minister Viktor Orban of Hungary, ambassadors were meeting to discuss a key step on the way to disbursing the much-needed money.

articles.title: British Army uses civilian ferry to take more than 1,400 troops to Europe
articles.summary: The British Army has used a civilian ferry to transport over 1,400 troops to mainland Europe for the first time in a generation. The move was likely a logistical solution to meet military needs.

articles.title: UN दूत बोलीं-भारत ने इजराइल की मदद कर कानून तोड़ा: हथियार भेजना नियमों के खिलाफ, इससे ग्लोबल सिस्टम
articles.summary: यूएन दूत ने कहा कि भारत ने इजराइल की मदद कर कानून तोड़ा। हथियार भेजना नियमों के खिलाफ है।

hi.title: UN दूत बोलीं-भारत ने इजराइल की मदद कर कानून तोड़ा: हथियार भेजना नियमों के खिलाफ, इससे ग्लोबल सिस्टम
hi.summary: यूएन दूत ने कहा कि भारत ने इजराइल की मदद कर कानून तोड़ा। हथियार भेजना नियमों के खिलाफ है।

articles.title: अमेरिका की ओर से युद्धविराम बढ़ाने पर अरब मीडिया में क्या कहा जा रहा है?
articles.summary: अमेरिकी राष्ट्रपति डोनाल्ड ट्रंप ने ईरान के साथ युद्धविराम को आगे बढ़ा दिया है। इस पर अरब जगत के मीडिया ने प्रतिक्रिया दी है। मीडिया ने इस निर्णय के पीछे के कारणों और इसके प्रभावों पर चर्चा की है।

articles.title: ईरान ने कैसे हासिल की ड्रोन तकनीक में महारत
articles.summary: ईरान ने ड्रोन तकनीक में महारत हासिल की है। इसका मकसद दुश्मन की रक्षा व्यवस्था को थकाना और उसके बजट पर दबाव बनाना है। ईरान इसी रणनीति के तहत अपने ड्रोन कार्यक्रम में निवेश करता रहा है।

articles.title: ट्रंप बोले, 'मुनीर और शरीफ़' के कहने पर बढ़ाया सीज़फ़ायर, मंगलवार को वॉशिंगटन में क्या-क्या हुआ
articles.summary: अमेरिकी राष्ट्रपति ट्रंप ने सीज़फ़ायर बढ़ाने की घोषणा की, जिसका उन्होंने दावा किया कि 'मुनीर और शरीफ़' के कहने पर किया गया। यह घोषणा वॉशिंगटन में मंगलवार को हुई। पाकिस्तान की राजधानी इस्लामाबाद में ईरान और अमेरिका के बीच दूसरे दौर की बातचीत होने वाली थी, लेकिन वार्ता नहीं हो पाई और ट्रंप ने युद्धविराम बढ़ाने की घोषणा कर दी.

articles.title: भारत ने संरा महासचिव पद की उम्मीदवार बाचेलेत से सुरक्षा परिषद सुधार के लक्ष्यों पर सवाल किए
articles.summary: भारत ने संयुक्त राष्ट्र महासचिव पद की उम्मीदवार बाचेलेत से सुरक्षा परिषद सुधार के लक्ष्यों पर सवाल किए। भारत ने उनसे उनकी प्राथमिकताओं और सुधार के तरीकों के बारे में जानकारी मांगी। यह सवाल भारत की सुरक्षा परिषद में सुधार की मांग को दर्शाता है।

articles.title: शांति वार्ता की मेज पर लौट सकते हैं दोनों देश, इस्लामाबाद में सुरक्षा सख्त
